name: Lima Cómo Vamos
description: Citizen observatory tracking urban quality of life in Lima through independent indicators and annual surveys.

name: Ocupa tu Calle
description: Citizen urbanism initiative activating neglected public spaces through community-led tactical interventions. UN-Habitat & Avina supported.

name: Sistema Urbano
description: Latin American ecosystem of urban projects led by Mariana Alegre Escorza; coordinates Lima Cómo Vamos, Ocupa tu Calle, Clima Urbano and Nodal.

name: Nodal
description: Latin American Urban Nodes — a community of collective urbanism connecting professionals, students, public workers and activists across the region.

name: Clima Urbano
description: Urban climate action program within the Sistema Urbano ecosystem focused on climate adaptation at the neighborhood scale.

name: Bogotá Cómo Vamos
description: Founding member of the Cómo Vamos network; monitors quality of life indicators across Bogotá since 1998.

name: Medellín Cómo Vamos
description: Citizen monitoring of Medellín's urban transformation and the city's internationally recognised social urbanism model.

name: Cali Cómo Vamos
description: Quality-of-life observatory for Cali and its metropolitan area; independent indicators and citizen perception surveys.

name: Barranquilla Cómo Vamos
description: Citizen observatory monitoring urban quality in Barranquilla with growing attention to climate and coastal issues.

name: Cartagena Cómo Vamos
description: Observatory tracking indicators in Cartagena with a focus on inequality and coastal vulnerability.

name: Manizales Cómo Vamos
description: Andean city observatory with emphasis on seismic and climate resilience in a mountainous urban context.

name: Quito Cómo Vamos
description: Ecuador's branch of the Cómo Vamos citizen-monitoring network measuring urban quality of life.

name: La Paz Cómo Vamos
description: Bolivian citizen observatory monitoring municipal quality of life and local-government performance.

name: Red Cómo Vamos
description: Regional network of 15+ citizen observatories monitoring quality of life across Latin American cities.

name: Lugares Públicos
description: Mexico City placemaking collective; co-organises regional Placemaking Latinoamérica conferences.

name: Placemaking Brasil
description: Pioneering place-led development network active in São Paulo and Brasília; links civic and private-sector actors.

name: Placemaking Latinoamérica
description: Regional conference network convening placemakers across Latin America to share practice and vision.

name: Espacio Lúdico
description: Chilean placemaking collective that convened the first Placemaking Latinoamérica gathering.

name: Mobilab
description: São Paulo mobility innovation lab operating inside the municipal government to prototype citizen-centred transport solutions.

name: Laboratorio para la Ciudad
description: First city-innovation lab in the Americas; inspired a continent-wide civic-lab movement.

name: Instituto Pólis
description: Research and advocacy NGO working on the right to the city and urban policy; one of Brazil's oldest urban-policy organisations.

name: IPPUC
description: Institute of Urban Research and Planning of Curitiba — foundational reference on bus rapid transit and integrated city planning.

name: Ciudad Viva
description: Citizen-led planning institution born from Santiago's anti-highway movement; prize-winning work on participatory planning.

name: ACIJ
description: Asociación Civil por la Igualdad y la Justicia — litigation, research and advocacy for the right to the city.

name: Corporación Ciudad Accesible
description: Accessibility advocacy for Chilean public space and transport; influences national accessibility standards.

name: Minha Sampa
description: Citizen mobilisation platform for São Paulo urban policy; runs campaigns and legislative pressure.

name: IMPLAN Guadalajara
description: Metropolitan Planning Institute for Greater Guadalajara — technical body coordinating nine municipalities.

name: LATINNO
description: Open database and network documenting democratic innovations in Latin America; 3500+ cases catalogued.

name: Fundación Avina
description: Latin American foundation funding civic innovation and democratic experiments across the region; long-standing Ocupa tu Calle and Nodal partner.

name: Claudia López Hernández
description: Former mayor of Bogotá (2020–2023); advanced the city's first metro line, mobility electrification and gender-equity urban policy.

name: Enrique Peñalosa Londoño
description: Two-term mayor of Bogotá; architect of TransMilenio BRT and an international reference on bus-rapid-transit and pedestrian-first cities.

name: Jorge Muñoz Wells
description: Former mayor of Lima (2019–2022); led recovery of public space, cycling infrastructure and the city's mobility master plan.

name: Mariana Alegre Escorza
description: Urbanist and social entrepreneur; founder of Lima Cómo Vamos, Ocupa tu Calle and Sistema Urbano. HGSD Loeb Fellow. Leads the Nodal community.

name: Gabriella Gómez-Mont
description: Founder of Laboratorio para la Ciudad (CDMX) and Experimentalista; works with cities worldwide on civic imagination and city innovation.

name: Jorge Pérez-Jaramillo
description: Architect-urbanist, ex-Planning Director of Medellín; international consultant on social urbanism and territorial strategy.

name: Tembici
description: Latin America's largest bike-share operator; runs Bike Sampa, Bike Rio, Bike Itaú and micro-mobility networks in 10+ cities.

name: Rappi
description: Colombian-born super-app; its courier and micro-mobility network has reshaped last-mile urban logistics across 9 countries.

name: Grupo IDOM Latam
description: International engineering and consultancy firm with a strong LatAm urban and transport practice; partners with IDB and city governments.

name: Estudio Lofft
description: Buenos Aires-based urban design and placemaking studio; projects across housing, public space and tactical urbanism in LatAm.

name: Julio Castillo
description: Urban researcher focusing on civic infrastructure and public space at the National University.

name: María Luisa Fernandez
description: Professor of Urban Planning at USP; researcher of micro-mobility and citizen transport.
